title: werauhia ringens
intro: Werauhia ringens, also known as the 'Ringed Werauhia', is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and protect against infections. The health benefits of Werauhia ringens include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, and it can also be used in topical applications, such as poultices and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
